Operation 'expensifyIntegrations' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. The current description lacks critical information for AI agent decision-making. It doesn't explain what the operation returns, the side effects of different job types, or how to interpret results. The summary is generic. Enhanced version specifies each job type's purpose, clarifies the response structure, and documents state-changing side effects to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: All Expensify API operations are performed via this single endpoint. The job type is specified in the requestJobDescription JSON parameter. Supported job types: file (export), reconciliation, download, create, update, get.
data.summary_suggestion: Execute Expensify integration job by type
data.description_suggestion: Executes an Expensify integration job through a unified endpoint. The operation type is determined by the jobType field in the requestJobDescription payload. Supported job types include: file (export expense reports), reconciliation (reconcile expenses), download (retrieve data), create (create reports/expenses), update (modify existing records), and get (retrieve records). Returns an IntegrationResponse object containing job status, results, and any errors. Side effects vary by job type: create/update operations modify expense data; export operations generate downloadable files; reconciliation operations update account state.
data.reasoning: The current description lacks critical information for AI agent decision-making. It doesn't explain what the operation returns, the side effects of different job types, or how to interpret results. The summary is generic. Enhanced version specifies each job type's purpose, clarifies the response structure, and documents state-changing side effects to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation and what to expect.
